objective: Make bilig decisively better than Google Sheets and Microsoft Excel, targeting at least 10x superiority across major spreadsheet/workbook categories.

requiredForBlanketTenXClaim: direct Google Sheets evidence for every objective category
direct Microsoft Excel evidence for every objective category
10x mean and p95 wins for every performance category
Excel-compatible correctness coverage across the committed formula and workbook semantics surface
browser UI responsiveness artifacts for large collaborative workbooks
security and reliability artifacts that cover production deployment behavior

categories.title: Calculation Correctness
categories.target: Excel-compatible semantics on the supported workbook and formula surface, with oracle-backed production routing.
currentEvidence: canonical formula closure is 300/300 (100%)
Office-listed formula breadth is 508/508 (100%)
tracked formula breadth is 525/525 (100%)
strategic canonical rows are production-routed; open canonical rows: 0
blockers: no generated scorecard currently compares all committed semantics directly against live Google Sheets and Microsoft Excel

categories.title: Recalculation Speed
categories.target: 10x mean and p95 wins on named recalculation workloads against each comparison target.
currentEvidence: dirty-execution: WorkPaper 5/5, HyperFormula 0/5; worst mean ratio 0.7022729674628281 on single-formula-edit-recalc; worst p95 ratio 0.7008869230220635 on partial-recompute-mixed-frontier
batch-edit: WorkPaper 6/6, HyperFormula 0/6; worst mean ratio 0.6445711003548408 on batch-suspended-multi-column; worst p95 ratio 0.7454666392938814 on batch-edit-single-column-with-undo
rebuild: WorkPaper 1/1, HyperFormula 0/1; worst mean ratio 0.24952910236150572 on rebuild-and-recalculate; worst p95 ratio 0.2409923766794544 on rebuild-and-recalculate
overall HyperFormula comparable scorecard is 46/46
blockers: current checked-in benchmark is against HyperFormula, not Google Sheets or Microsoft Excel
only 2 comparable HyperFormula workloads are 10x wins on both mean and p95

categories.title: Structural Edit Performance
categories.target: 10x mean and p95 wins for insert/delete/move rows and columns at workbook scale.
currentEvidence: structural-rows: WorkPaper 3/3, HyperFormula 0/3; worst mean ratio 0.7095183752254048 on structural-delete-rows; worst p95 ratio 0.7249915535641869 on structural-delete-rows
structural-columns: WorkPaper 3/3, HyperFormula 0/3; worst mean ratio 0.8642535647861759 on structural-insert-columns; worst p95 ratio 0.871853534177693 on structural-insert-columns
blockers: structural rows and columns lead HyperFormula, but the worst ratios are not 10x
no direct Sheets or Excel structural-edit timing artifact exists in the repo
